title: DOM State, Events, and Accessible Interaction · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Build a keyboard-operable service-status interface whose DOM, focus, announcements, and visual state stay synchronized. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an accessible interactive service-status interface after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Query, create, move, replace, and remove DOM nodes with explicit missing-element and ownership handling.
Derive DOM output from application state without duplicating conflicting sources of truth.
Trace event target, currentTarget, capture, bubble, composed paths, and cancellation before delegating behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Dom Query Create evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Dom Query Create assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: An accessible status filter has 7 options, works by keyboard and pointer, survives dynamic list updates through delegation, restores focus after removal, and announces the changed result count. Competency probe: Query, create, move, replace, and remove DOM nodes with explicit missing-element and ownership handling.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Dom Query Create has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
